author: potuz
content:
Honestly I don't know how the rest client handles honest reorgs but notice the timelines of the Payload by itself is not all. This is his I would do it (which mimics Prysm when my PR merges). Assuming you're the proposer next slot

1. Listen to the head event. 
2. If the head goes empty -> full and it happens timely send FCU with arguments. 
3. If the head goes empty -> full and it's not timely withhold FCU.
4. Listen to see what the PTC voted. If you sent FCU and PTC doesn't have a quorum for late, keep your FCU and build on full. Regardless of PTC 
4. If you sent FCU but PTC has quorum for late, here, and only here, send another FCU to roll back the EL and build on empty. 
5. If you didn't send FCU and PTC doesn't have a quorum for early, keep your bet and try to build on empty. 
6. If you didn't send FCU but PTC has a quorum for early, send and FCU and try to build on full. 

Repeat the last 4 points at the beginning of the slot right before proposing

author: potuz
content: I think we need to agree on the difference between a quorum for late and a lack of quorum for early. My interpretation is as above

author: nflaig
content: so be clear, that's only if your are the proposer of next slot? in that case, makes sense

author: nflaig
content: but not sure it's entirely relevant or applicable for `head_v2` emission

author: potuz
content: Exactly, head emission is independent of all the above and it happened in step 1, it is emitted when the Payload has been processed and the validator can check the timeliness then

author: potuz
content: Then later it can query for PTC on a different endpoint perhaps

author: terencechain
content: i think i have implemented sending the rollback FCU as a feature flag for those users that may prefer that UX, not sure if it got merged

author: sproul
content: I don't know about value, but to me this just accurately encodes how fork choice works. We run fork choice after receiving a block or payload, so it's very natural for us to emit events if the head changes for either of these

author: sproul
content: for me it's more work thinking about how and why to supress the natural output of `get_head`, which is now a block root and a payload status

author: sproul
content: I'm not planning to make LH withold fcU in any circumstances, it feels like a hack and I'm going to see if we can get by without doing that.

author: sproul
content: that algorithm seems over-complicated to me compared to: "tell the EL what the head is", it can deal with receiving a reversion fcU, because it has to deal with this in the general case anyway.

author: chonghe
content:
From the discussion here: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/590#discussion_r3026684195
Some EL tools may need the payload status as they change, and also builder may need it (I am not sure about this)

Regarding the reorg/payload status changes back and forth from full to empty, that's not specified in the description of the `head_v2` event, and that's intentional, as it could be confusing/cloud the description. Maybe clients can implement that as they wish? But the second emission is specified and should be included as the payload status changes from `empty` to `full`

author: sproul
content: The [validator](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/gloas/validator.md#executionpayload) spec uses `should_build_on_full`, which enshrines the behaviour "build on full unless late/unavailable has quorum"

author: sproul
content: which I think is compatible with your algorithm?

author: potuz
content: I hope it didn't or I'll have a conflict in the Payload reorg one 🙂

author: potuz
content: Yep, it's no coincidence, I wrote `should_build_on_full`

author: potuz
content: I think it's fine to rollback if needed, but withholding the FCU ended up being easier to implement for us

author: potuz
content: The only thing I think we should agree on is on not sending that rollback unless it's with attributes

author: sproul
content: why tho? if the EL is likely going to have to reorg we may as well tell them sooner rather than later

author: sproul
content: I really prefer to keep this as simple as possible

author: sproul
content: I can understand not wanting to create churn as an optimisation, but I don’t think this optimisation needs to be mandated does it?

author: potuz
content: Because it creates a new problem downstream on consumers: rollbacks of the chain do not exist. Dapps and their front ends may be already programmed counting with this. We would open Pandora's box if we now need to get every user's input over this. The guard is absolutely trivial: if you're head is changing full -> empty it cannot happen at payload insertion so this is really concentrated on proposal time: PTC does not change the head of attesters

author: potuz
content: If the head changes full-> empty because of attestations at skipped slots then that affects attesters but it's trivial to avoid that FCU, at least for us we just don't ever send that FCU even for entry->full unless we are proposing
